message:
test(connectors): expand 15 connectors from 140-148 range to 160+ tests

All connectors now at 161+:
- reddit 148→163, pandadoc 148→165, elasticsearch 148→183
- pulumi 147→177, hubspot 147→179, google-ai 146→171
- datadog 146→202, twitter 145→186, gmail 145→177
- google-calendar 144→174, qdrant 143→161, posthog 143→181
- intercom 143→181, grafana 142→183, todoist 140→191

All tests pass, clippy clean with -D warnings.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-10r4k","title":"FIX: SymbolResponseBuilder was_bounded false positive when symbols exactly equal limit","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-03-05T02:04:38.754934Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-03-05T02:04:49.337106Z","closed_at":"2026-03-05T02:04:49.337094Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["correctness","mesh"],"comments":[{"id":733,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-10r4k","author":"Jeff Emanuel","text":"Root cause: add_from_repair_engine() used `available_count >= max_response_symbols` to detect bounding, but `select_symbols()` already caps its return at `max_response_symbols`, making `available_count == max_response_symbols` trigger even when all available symbols were returned.

Fix: Moved was_bounded computation to `build()` where `total_available` (the true object store count) is known. Now `was_bounded = sent_count < remaining` using the actual counts rather than relying on the post-selection count from the engine.

All 508 mesh tests pass (425 unit + 81 integration + 2 doc). The previously-failing `meshnode_symbol_request_is_final_when_all_sent` now passes, and the existing `response_builder_marks_bounded_when_request_limit_hits` continues to pass.","created_at":"2026-03-05T02:04:49Z"}]}
 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-11g2","title":"E2E-LOG-SCHEMA: JSONL schema + validator + log tooling","description":"# E2E-LOG-SCHEMA: JSONL schema + validator + log tooling

## Goal
Define a single structured logging schema for E2E scripts and fcp-e2e harness output, plus a validator.

## Scope
- JSONL schema (fields: timestamp, script, step, correlation_id, duration_ms, result, artifacts, error_code, details).
- Validator tool or test that fails if logs are malformed.
- Documented guidelines for step logging.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Schema is documented under docs/testing/.
- Validator runs in CI or as part of e2e runner.
- All existing e2e scripts conform to the schema.
","notes":"Script E2E runners now enforce schema validation: added requirement for fcp-e2e and call to validate log JSONL at end of each scripts/e2e/*.sh. Updated denial_path to structured pass/fail logs with correlation_id/duration_ms/details; moved extra fields into details for revocation_flow and taint_approval.

[AlienArtifactPractical]
- Add evidence ledger fields with per-check log-odds contributions and explicit loss decision.
- Add anytime-valid risk control (e-process) for approvals/denials and record epsilon in receipts.
[ExtremeOptimizationPractical]
- Compile policies to DAG/BDD and cache allow/deny results with LRU.
- Use bitset capability sets for fast subset checks.

 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-12ke","title":"[IDEA-23.1] Implement lifecycle state machine + canary policy","description":"## What
Implement lifecycle state tracking and canary rollout logic in `fcp-host`.

## How
- Add persistent lifecycle records (mesh object or host state).
- Health-based promotion/rollback thresholds.
- APIs for `promote`, `rollback`, and `status`.

## Success Criteria
- Canary failure triggers rollback with audit event.

## Acceptance Criteria (Refined)
- Lifecycle state persists across restarts.
- State transitions are auditable and logged.
","notes":"[AlienArtifactPractical]
- Add spectral routing score (effective resistance / Laplacian) as optional metric and log chosen path.
- Set gossip TTL from measured mixing-time; add convergence test that asserts bound.
[ExtremeOptimizationPractical]
- Use SmallVec for usually-small object/symbol lists to reduce allocations.
- Batch symbol requests and use Bytes for zero-copy in hot paths.

 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-13ii","title":"Fix CredentialObject wildcard host_allow semantics","description":"CredentialObject::is_host_allowed treats '*.example.com' as matching base domain 'example.com', but egress/credential injection expect wildcard to be subdomain-only. Align semantics to avoid unexpected root-domain access.","notes":"[AlienArtifactPractical]
- Add non-interference checks: policy compile -> reachability graph -> deny proofs.
- Add transparency log membership proof storage for attestations (audit-friendly).
[ExtremeOptimizationPractical]
- Cache compiled sandbox policies to avoid rebuilds.
- Stream verification to reduce peak memory usage.

 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-lszk.45.3.5","title":"fcp.workspace-events: Google Workspace Events connector plus Pub/Sub lifecycle management","description":"Implement the Google Workspace Events connector on top of the shared Google platform, including explicit provisioning and lifecycle handling for Workspace subscriptions and Pub/Sub-backed delivery paths.","design":"Workspace Events is the event-driven keystone of the Google Workspace ecosystem. Unlike simpler CRUD services, it spans API operations plus external Pub/Sub resources and renewal/reactivation workflows. The connector should therefore use the shared substrate for generic Google mechanics but expect a larger handwritten overlay for subscription lifecycle, event consumption, and provisioning integration.","acceptance_criteria":"- Subscription and reactivation surfaces are defined and policy-mapped.
- Pub/Sub lifecycle requirements are documented and integrated with provisioning automation.
- Event payload semantics are explicit enough for agent-facing introspection and tests.","notes":"This connector is strategically important because it unlocks reactive Google workflows instead of limiting the platform to polling and request/response automation.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-03-06T07:31:18.279048Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-03-06T07:40:18.348907Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["connectors","events","fcp2","google","workspace"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-lszk.45.3.5","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-lszk.45.1.5.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-06T07:40:16.940992Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-lszk.45.3.5","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-lszk.45.1.8.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-06T07:40:17.057933Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-lszk.45.3.5","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-lszk.45.2.6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-06T07:31:27.290238Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-lszk.45.3.5","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-lszk.45.3","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-06T07:31:18.279048Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":899,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-lszk.45.3.5","author":"Jeff Emanuel","text":"Workspace Events was explicitly called out in the memo as one of the best candidates for thin high-value helpers and provisioning integration. It is not just another CRUD connector; it spans subscriptions, renew/reactivate flows, and Pub/Sub lifecycle management, which is why it now depends on the helper-policy and provisioning-detail beads as well as the overall migration acceptance gate.","created_at":"2026-03-06T07:40:18Z"}]}
 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-lszk.45.3.6","title":"fcp.people: Google People/Contacts connector via the shared discovery-pinned substrate","description":"Implement the Google People/Contacts connector using the shared Google platform, with careful privacy and approval treatment around personal data access and mutation.","design":"People is mechanically well-suited to the shared platform, but the policy layer matters more than usual because the data is often personal and sensitive. The connector should make capability and approval boundaries especially explicit for contact mutation and profile reads.","acceptance_criteria":"- Core People operations are generator-backed.
- Privacy-sensitive reads and writes are mapped clearly into FCP approval/risk semantics.
- The connector is usable without hiding policy-sensitive behavior behind convenience wrappers.","notes":"People is lower priority than Drive/Docs/Sheets/Chat, but important for personal-assistant and coordination workflows.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-03-06T07:31:18.512657Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-03-06T07:31:27.401570Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["connectors","fcp2","google","people","workspace"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-lszk.45.3.6","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-lszk.45.2.6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-06T07:31:27.401537Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-lszk.45.3.6","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-lszk.45.3","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-06T07:31:18.512657Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-lszk.45.3.7","title":"fcp.admin-reports: Google Workspace Admin Reports connector via the shared discovery-pinned substrate","description":"Implement the Google Workspace Admin Reports connector using the shared Google platform, with explicit treatment of admin-only scopes, domain-admin environments, and audit/reporting semantics.","design":"Admin Reports is valuable because it exposes audit logs and usage reports, but it also represents the sharp edge of Google admin-only scope behavior. The connector should therefore be a proving ground for how the shared platform handles admin-only scopes, restricted environments, and domain-specific deployment guidance without pretending every Google connector targets the same user context.","acceptance_criteria":"- Admin-only scope behavior is documented and policy-mapped.
- Generated baseline operations are combined with explicit deployment and approval guidance.
- The connector's assumptions about domain-admin context are obvious to operators and agents.","notes":"This connector is intentionally included early in the wave because it forces the platform to handle the reality that not all Google APIs are personal-user friendly.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-03-06T07:31:18.836160Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-03-06T07:31:27.512682Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["admin","connectors","fcp2","google","workspace"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-lszk.45.3.7","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-lszk.45.2.6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-06T07:31:27.512649Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-lszk.45.3.7","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-lszk.45.3","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-06T07:31:18.836160Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-lszk.5","title":"STANDARD: Connector Spec Template (FCP2)","description":"# STANDARD: Connector Spec Template (FCP2)


## Goal
Provide the canonical copy/paste Beads template for planning V2-only connectors (single-zone, default deny, constraints, receipts/audit, tests/E2E).

## Purpose
A copy/paste template for planning **new FCP2 connectors** in Beads.

Goal: every connector plan should be mechanically consistent with FCP2 (single-zone binding, default deny, NetworkConstraints, receipts/audit, taint/approval), and should include tests/E2E from day one.

This template is intentionally independent of any specific external service.

---

## Required Bead Structure (recommended)
For each connector **epic**:
1. `<connector>.1` — **Manifest + Capability Map (FCP2)**
2. `<connector>.2` — **Provisioning Automation** (OAuth/webhooks/setup)
3. `<connector>.3` — **TEST: Unit/Integration Tests (mock-only)**
4. `<connector>.4` — **E2E: Connector Compliance Run**

For each connector **feature** bead under the epic (operations / slices):
- Add dependency: feature → `<connector>.1` (manifest/caps)
- Add dependency on `flywheel_connectors-1n78.33` if the feature requires **persistent cursor/dedupe/streaming state**.

---

## Template: Connector Epic Description

### 1) Goal
- One sentence: what this connector enables.

### 2) Hard Requirements (FCP2)
- **Single-zone binding** (exactly one `ZoneId` per connector instance).
- **Default deny**: every operation requires explicit CapabilityTokens.
- **Sandbox + NetworkConstraints** enforced mechanically.
- **Receipts + audit** for all writes/dangerous ops.
- **No secrets on disk**; strict log redaction.
- **Prefer secretless connectors**: credentials injected at the network boundary via `CredentialObject`/`CredentialId` (egress proxy) so raw secret bytes never enter connector memory.

### 3) Manifest Archetypes (closed set)
List which apply (only from):
- `bidirectional`, `streaming`, `operational`, `storage`, `knowledge`

(“webhook/polling/request-response/database” are interaction patterns, not archetypes.)

### 4) External Surface (mechanical)
- NetworkConstraints allowlist (default deny): which hosts are permitted.
- Redirect policy (default deny to new hosts).
- Timeouts and max response sizes.

### 5) Capability Model
- Capability families and per-operation required caps.
- **Rule**: capability IDs MUST NOT encode hostnames/ports.

### 6) State Model
Declare one:
- `stateless`
- `singleton_writer` (requires lease)
- `crdt` (rare; justify)

List what state is persisted (dedupe keys, cursors, mapping tables) and how to bound it.

### 7) Security / Taint / Approval
- Identify tainted inputs (webhooks, external content, untrusted metadata).
- Define what requires ApprovalToken vs is safe.

### 8) Observability
- Structured JSON logs with correlation IDs.
- Reason codes for denials.

### 9) Testing Strategy (required)
- Unit/integration tests are **mock-only**.
- E2E compliance run uses the shared runner and produces evidence bundle.

### 10) Acceptance Criteria
- “Passes compliance runner (static + dynamic).”
- “No real external calls in unit tests.”
- “Dangerous ops audited/receipted and approval gated.”

---

## Template: Manifest + Capability Map Bead (`<connector>.1`)
- Full operation list + schema IDs
- Capability families
- NetworkConstraints per operation
- Sandbox profile requirements
- Good/bad manifest examples for static compliance

---

## Template: Provisioning Bead (`<connector>.2`)
- Auth setup (preferred): create `CredentialObject` + `CredentialId` and rely on MeshNode egress-proxy credential injection ("secretless" connectors)
- Webhook/OAuth automation
- `fcp doctor` readiness checks
- Fully mockable for unit tests

---

## Template: Test Bead (`<connector>.3`)
- Mock servers for external API
- Golden vectors for:
  - signature validation
  - redaction
  - idempotency
  - pagination
  - streaming bounds/backpressure

---

## Template: E2E Compliance Bead (`<connector>.4`)
- Run shared compliance runner
- Validate:
  - default deny
  - NetworkConstraints
  - receipts/audit
  - structured logs
- Capture artifacts bundle
","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-15T09:56:47.053051026Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-15T15:54:45.029007874Z","closed_at":"2026-01-15T15:54:45.029007874Z","close_reason":"Closed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-lszk.5","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-lszk","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-02T18:31:41Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-lszk.6","title":"fcp.pulumi — Infrastructure as Code Connector","description":"# fcp.pulumi — Infrastructure as Code Connector


## Goal
Provide an FCP2-compliant Pulumi connector for infrastructure management (preview/apply) with approval gating and auditable execution.

## Overview
The **fcp.pulumi** connector provides a safety-critical interface between agents and Pulumi infrastructure management.

It wraps the Pulumi CLI (and/or Pulumi Automation API later) to enable:
- safe inspection of stacks
- deterministic previews
- tightly controlled updates

This connector treats infra operations with appropriate gravity:
- read operations are safe by default
- updates/destroys require explicit approval + strict idempotency
- destructive operations require stricter multi-approval patterns

## Manifest archetypes (closed set)
- `operational`

> Interaction patterns (non-archetype): Request/Response (CLI-based)

## Operations (initial)
### Safe (read-only)
- `stack.list`
- `stack.select`
- `stack.outputs.read`
- `state.export` (bounded; redacted)
- `preview` (no side effects)

### Risky/Dangerous (writes)
- `up` / `apply` (approval gated)
- `destroy` (multi-approval; dangerous)
- `refresh` (may be risky depending on provider)

## Zone Architecture

<!-- fcp2-single-zone-note -->
**FCP2 single-zone binding note:** A connector instance binds to exactly one `ZoneId` for its entire lifetime.
The *"zones"* described in this section are a **recommended deployment pattern** (run separate connector instances in separate zones with different capability grants/policies) and/or conceptual risk tiers.
The connector MUST NOT multiplex multiple zones internally.

Recommended zones:
- `z:infra:pulumi:plan` (read + preview)
- `z:infra:pulumi:apply` (up/apply)
- `z:infra:pulumi:destroy` (destroy)

## FCP2 Hard Requirements
- Single-zone binding.
- Default deny via capabilities.
- Sandbox profile must forbid arbitrary filesystem access outside an explicit working directory.
- NetworkConstraints default deny (CLI should not talk to arbitrary hosts unless explicitly permitted).
- Strict receipts/audit for any external side effects.
- No secrets on disk; Pulumi secrets must be injected via `CredentialId` and never logged.

## Tests
- Mock-only unit/integration tests (no real cloud providers).
- Golden tests for:
  - safety tier classification
  - log redaction
  - idempotency + intent/receipt behavior

## Success Criteria
- Epic expanded into base beads `.1`..`.4` per `flywheel_connectors-lszk.5` before implementation begins.
- Passes mechanical connector compliance runner (`flywheel_connectors-1n78.21.5`) and E2E framework (`flywheel_connectors-e3i9`).

 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-lszk.6.1","title":"fcp.pulumi: Manifest + Capability Map (FCP2)","description":"# fcp.pulumi: Manifest + Capability Map (FCP2)

## Goal
Define the complete FCP2 manifest surface for `fcp.pulumi`:
- operation list + schemas
- capability families
- sandbox profile
- NetworkConstraints (deny-by-default)
- state model (if any)

## Notes
- Capability IDs MUST NOT encode hostnames/ports; hosts belong in NetworkConstraints.
- Writes (`up`/`destroy`) must be Strict idempotency + receipts/audit.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Manifest validates under strict rules.
- Mechanical compliance runner accepts the manifest.
